selection: Fifty recurring ligand scaffold families selected by breadth of use across homogeneous catalysis reviews, ligand databases, and privileged-chiral-ligand literature. Entries are editable scaffold prototypes, not claims that one named commercial ligand represents the whole family.
policy: Preserve donor topology and literature-conventional orientation. Expand variable sites from the supplied source. Tetrahedral chirality is encoded and CIP-read back; axial, spiro, and ferrocene-planar chirality requires native ChemDraw/literature review and must never be mirrored automatically.

sources.title: Kraken: A Large-Scale Ligand Knowledge Base for Monodentate P-Donor Ligands
Ligand Knowledge Base for Bidentate P,P- and P,N-Donor Ligands
Oxazoline Ligands
sources.role: BOX, PyBOX, PyOx, and PHOX families

sources.title: N-Heterocyclic Carbene and Mesoionic Carbene Ligands
Pincer Ligands
sources.role: PCP, PNP, POCOP, and NCN donor topologies

variable_sites: all three aryl rings and their substitution patterns
entries.orientation: Place P at the visual hub and fan the three aryl groups evenly; keep the principal substituted aryl ring horizontal when the source distinguishes one arm.
Use a balanced three-arm fan around P; do not imply stereochemistry unless the three carbon substituents are constitutionally different and the source specifies it.
Draw the biaryl axis left-to-right with P on the lower or outer ortho position, following the cited reaction panel; keep the remote aryl ring unobstructed.

chirality.class: possible atropisomerism; not asserted by parent prototype

entries.orientation: Fan the three P-O bonds evenly and show every intervening oxygen explicitly.
Show two P-O arms and the P-N arm distinctly; for BINOL-derived members preserve the literature axial orientation and source stereolabel.

chirality.class: often axial and/or P-stereogenic; parent prototype unspecified

entries.orientation: Use a shallow P-CH2-P chelate V with both P donors on the same visual side when drawn as a free ligand.
Keep P-CH2-CH2-P as an open zigzag with the two donor atoms visually accessible; avoid folding the P arms over the backbone.
Use an extended zigzag P-(CH2)3-P chain and keep the P arms outside the carbon-chain envelope.
Use a regular benzene with both P donors at adjacent top or side vertices and directed outward without ring distortion.
Draw the xanthene core horizontally with O centered low or high and the two P donors symmetrically at the outer aryl positions; preserve the wide-bite visual span.
Place the ether oxygen near the center and open the two phenyl rings into a broad V; position the P donors at the outside ortho sites.
Use ChemDraw's conventional ferrocene depiction with aligned Cp rings and Fe centered; place the two P arms at corresponding 1,1' positions.

chirality.class: possible planar chirality after unsymmetrical substitution

entries.representation_limit: Disconnected MOL fragments cannot encode eta-5 ferrocene bonding faithfully; reconstruct in native ChemDraw.

entries.orientation: Place the biaryl axis near horizontal and the two P donors on the inner 2,2' positions; retain the source's conventional axial hand and never mirror for packing.
Keep the biphenyl axis horizontal with inner P donors facing the chelation pocket; preserve any source-defined atropisomeric hand.

names: 联苯二氧亚甲基双膦骨架
entries.orientation: Draw the fused dioxole-capped biaryl core horizontally with the two P donors facing the central chelation region; never mirror an assigned atropisomer.
Use a central horizontal o-phenylene unit with matched phospholane rings on adjacent sites; keep all four source-defined stereocenters and wedge directions.
Keep the P-CH2-CH2-P bridge central and the two phospholane rings on opposite outer sides; preserve all source-defined tetrahedral stereocenters.
Draw the chiral C-C backbone as a clear zigzag between the P atoms and keep both methyl stereobonds visible without crossing P-aryl bonds.
Use native ChemDraw ferrocene notation with the planar-chiral substituted Cp ring explicit; place the side-chain P and ring-bound P in the literature-standard chelating orientation.
entries.representation_limit: A disconnected MOL cannot faithfully encode eta-5 bonding or ferrocene planar chirality.

entries.orientation: Place the aryl linker horizontally with P and oxazoline N facing the same chelation pocket; preserve the oxazoline stereocenter and avoid flipping the five-membered ring.
Draw the inter-ring bond horizontally and rotate both pyridine N atoms inward toward the same chelation cleft; use regular six-membered rings.
Use the conventional flat three-ring fused system with the two N atoms at the inner terminal positions and an open chelation bay.
Draw the three rings in an extended shallow arc with all three N atoms facing one common coordination side.
Keep N=C-C=N in an s-cis chelating arrangement with both N lone-pair directions visually inward; show E/Z geometry only if the source specifies it.
Use an open N-C-C-N zigzag with both N atoms at the outside ends and normal tetrahedral carbon angles.

names: 反式-1,2-二氨基环己烷骨架
entries.orientation: Use a regular 2D cyclohexane hexagon unless the paper uses a chair; place adjacent amino groups with explicit trans stereobonds and preserve the named enantiomer.
Draw two regular five-membered oxazoline rings symmetrically about the central bridge with both N atoms facing inward; preserve the matched stereocenters.
Center the pyridine horizontally and place matched oxazoline rings at both 2,6 positions; all three N donors must face the same coordination pocket.
Place pyridine and oxazoline side-by-side with both N donors inward; use a regular five-membered ring and preserve the stereobond.
Use the familiar nearly C2-symmetric open N2O2 pocket: salicylidene rings outside, O donors low/outward and imine N donors inward; preserve diamine stereochemistry.
Place the binaphthyl axis horizontal with the two 2,2'-O groups forming the inner pocket; preserve the cited axial hand and do not mirror to fit a panel.
Use the conventional orthogonal spirobiindane projection with the central spiro atom clear and both O groups readable; preserve the source's spiro hand.

names: 四芳基二氧戊环二甲醇骨架
entries.orientation: Keep the dioxolane ring as a regular five-membered core and distribute the four aryl groups without overlap; preserve both acetal stereocenters.
Draw the macrocycle as a balanced square-like ring with four pyrrole units and a centered N4 cavity; use consistent alternating double bonds and avoid squeezing one quadrant.
Use a symmetric O-C-C-C-O chelating arc and a consistent delocalized resonance choice; do not mix neutral diketone and anionic diketonate without source evidence.
Use a regular five-membered imidazolylidene ring with carbene carbon at the metal-facing vertex and both N substituents spreading away from that vertex.
Use a regular saturated five-membered ring with carbene carbon at the coordination vertex and preserve any 4,5 stereocenters from the source.
Use a regular five-membered triazolylidene ring, keep formal charge/resonance internally consistent, and place the carbene carbon at the metal-facing edge.

names: 环状烷基氨基卡宾骨架
entries.orientation: Draw the saturated five-membered ring with carbene carbon at the coordination vertex, N on one adjacent side, and the quaternary carbon on the other; avoid compressing its bond angles.

chirality.class: possible tetrahedral chirality in substituted members; parent unspecified

names: 芳基双膦钳形配体骨架
entries.orientation: Draw the aryl backbone horizontally with two P arms at flanking positions and both P donors aimed toward the same side as the central aryl-C donor.
Center the pyridine ring with N at the coordination side and arrange both phosphine arms symmetrically toward that same pocket.
Use a symmetric P-arm/N-center/P-arm W or shallow bowl with all three donors facing one coordination region and normal tetrahedral N/C angles.
Draw the aryl ring horizontally with two O-P arms on flanking positions; retain explicit O atoms and aim both P donors toward the central aryl-C coordination side.

names: 双胺甲基芳基钳形骨架
entries.orientation: Use a horizontal aryl backbone with two aminomethyl arms opening toward the same side as the central aryl-C donor; keep the N atoms outside crowded ring edges.
Use a regular pentagon with delocalized anionic Cp notation or the source's circle convention; when coordinated, center the metal over the ring face using native ChemDraw hapticity notation.
entries.representation_limit: MOL prototype records the anionic ring only, not eta-5 metal bonding.

entries.orientation: Draw a regular fused benzene/five-membered indenyl system with the five-membered ring facing the metal; use native hapticity notation for complexes.
entries.representation_limit: MOL prototype records the anionic ring only, not eta-5 metal bonding.

entries.orientation: Use an open N-C-C-C-N chelating arc with both N donors facing inward; for the anion, keep charge and double-bond resonance consistent with the cited drawing.
Place B at the upper or rear hub with three pyrazolyl arms forming a balanced tripod and all three distal N donors directed toward one metal-binding face.
Draw the C-C backbone centrally, fan phenyl groups away from the N-N chelation side, and preserve both source-defined stereocenters.
Use the same open C2-like N2O2 pocket as salen but show saturated C-N bonds; preserve the diamine stereocenters and normal amine bond angles.
